text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: Metastatic melanoma, BRAF V600E mutant. Prior treatment with dabrafenib/trametinib (discontinued secondary to disease progression at approximately 8 months), stereotactic radiosurgery to two intracranial lesions (right frontal and left cerebellar), followed by ipilimumab/nivolumab combination immunotherapy x2 cycles. Currently on steroid taper following grade 3 immune-mediated colitis with ipilimumab held. Restaging evaluation.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain from approximately 3 months prior (at time of progression on BRAF-targeted therapy).

---

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST

Lungs/Airways: No endobronchial lesions. No new pulmonary nodules. Previously noted small right lower lobe subpleural nodule measuring approximately 4 mm is again seen, essentially unchanged. No consolidation or pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum/Hila: Mediastinal and bilateral hilar soft tissue is within expected limits. No convincing pathologic mediastinal lymphadenopathy. A small subcarinal node measuring approximately 8 mm in short axis is slightly smaller compared to prior study (previously approximately 11 mm). A right paratracheal node is also slightly smaller.

Heart/Pericardium: Heart size normal. No pericardial effusion.

Chest Wall/Bones: No suspicious osseous lesions. No rib or vertebral lesion identified.

---

CT ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST

Liver: Multiple hypodense hepatic lesions are again demonstrated. There is heterogeneous response with some lesions showing interval decrease in size and others appearing essentially unchanged. The largest hepatic lesion in the right lobe measures approximately 3.4 cm in greatest dimension, decreased compared to 4.2 cm on the prior examination. A cluster of lesions in hepatic segments VI/VII appear stable. A small lesion in the left lobe (segment II/III) appears slightly less conspicuous but not definitively resolved. Overall hepatic tumor burden appears modestly decreased.

Spleen: Unremarkable. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Visualized portions appear unremarkable without focal lesion or ductal dilatation.

Adrenal Glands: No adrenal masses.

Kidneys/Ureters: No hydronephrosis. No renal lesions.

Gastrointestinal: Diffuse mild colonic wall thickening is noted, most prominent in the sigmoid and descending colon, which given the clinical history of recent immune-mediated colitis is consistent with colitis/post-inflammatory change. No free air or obstruction. Mild amount of fluid in the pelvis, nonspecific.

Lymph Nodes: Retroperitoneal and mesenteric lymph nodes are mildly prominent but slightly improved compared to prior imaging. A para-aortic node previously measuring approximately 14 mm in short axis now measures approximately 10 mm. Additional small mesenteric nodes are stable to mildly decreased.

Soft Tissues: Subcutaneous nodule previously identified in the right thigh region is no longer visualized on this examination. No other new subcutaneous lesions. Soft tissues otherwise unremarkable.

Pelvis: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. Uterus and adnexa appear normal for age. No pelvic free fluid of concern.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

Technique: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain performed with and without gadolinium contrast, including axial T1, T2, FLAIR, DWI, SWI, and post-contrast T1 sequences.

Findings:

Right Frontal Lobe: At the site of prior stereotactic radiosurgery, there is a small focus of T1 post-contrast enhancement measuring approximately 5 mm, markedly decreased from prior (estimated 8 mm on the examination performed at the time of SRS planning/prior restaging). The surrounding T2/FLAIR signal is minimal and stable. The appearance is most consistent with treatment effect/radiation change. No surrounding mass effect. No evidence of active tumor growth at this site.

Left Cerebellum: The previously treated left cerebellar lesion demonstrates no definite residual enhancement on this examination. There is minimal residual T2/FLAIR signal at this site. Overall appearance markedly improved compared to prior, consistent with favorable post-treatment change following SRS.

Remainder of Brain: No new foci of abnormal enhancement to suggest new intracranial metastatic disease. No new hemorrhagic lesions. No evidence of leptomeningeal involvement. The ventricles and sulci are normal in caliber. No midline shift. No restricted diffusion to suggest acute ischemia.

Sella/Skull Base: Unremarkable.

Orbit/Calvarium: No suspicious bony lesions.

---

LABORATORY VALUE OF NOTE (PROVIDED FOR CORRELATION): LDH 298 U/L (elevated above institutional upper limit of normal).

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Hepatic metastases — mixed but on balance slightly improved compared to prior. The dominant right lobe lesion has decreased in size (now approximately 3.4 cm, previously 4.2 cm). Other hepatic lesions are stable to mildly decreased in conspicuity. Some lesions appear largely unchanged, reflecting a heterogeneous intratumoral response.

2. Lymphadenopathy — mildly improved. Previously enlarged para-aortic and mediastinal nodes are slightly smaller.

3. Right thigh subcutaneous nodule — no longer identified, consistent with interval resolution.

4. Post-SRS treatment effect — previously treated right frontal and left cerebellar intracranial metastases show markedly decreased or absent enhancement with minimal residual signal change, consistent with favorable treatment response to prior radiosurgery. No new intracranial lesions.

5. Colonic wall thickening in the sigmoid and descending colon consistent with known history of immune-mediated colitis, without evidence of complication.

6. No new sites of metastatic disease identified.

Overall, findings suggest a mixed but generally favorable early response to systemic immunotherapy, with some hepatic lesions improved, others stable, and complete response in the previously treated brain lesions. LDH remains elevated, suggesting continued disease activity.

Radiologist: [Electronically signed]
Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging / Neuroradiology
